description: Emergency department visit note — chest pain workup, rule-out ACS, ST changes on ECG
parsed_text:
EMERGENCY DEPARTMENT VISIT NOTE
MetroGeneral Hospital — Emergency Department
Encounter ID: ED-2025-1103-004421
Date/Time: 2025-11-03 02:14

Patient: Patel, Vikram S.
DOB: 1957-03-18
MRN: MGH-0887453

Provider: Dr. Rebecca Okafor, MD, FACEP
NPI: 9988776655
Specialty: Emergency Medicine

---
CHIEF COMPLAINT
Chest pain, onset 1 hour ago, radiating to left arm.

SUBJECTIVE
68-year-old male brought by EMS for acute onset chest pain beginning approximately 1 hour PTA. Pain described as pressure-like, 7/10 severity, radiating to left arm and jaw. Associated with diaphoresis and mild dyspnea. History of HTN, hyperlipidemia, former smoker (quit 10 years ago). Takes amlodipine and atorvastatin at home. Denies fever, vomiting, syncope.

OBJECTIVE
Vitals on arrival:
  BP: 158/98 mmHg   HR: 102 bpm   Temp: 98.8°F   RR: 20 breaths/min
  SpO2: 94% on room air (improved to 98% on 4L O2 NC)   Weight: 196 lbs

Physical Exam: Diaphoretic, pale, mild distress. CV: Tachycardic, regular rhythm, no murmur. Lungs: Mild bibasilar crackles. Abdomen: Soft. No leg edema.

12-lead ECG: Sinus tachycardia. ST depression 1.5 mm leads V4-V6 and I, aVL. No ST elevation. No new LBBB.
Initial Troponin-I: 0.04 ng/mL (upper limit normal 0.03) — mildly elevated.
Chest X-ray: No acute infiltrate, mild cardiomegaly.

ASSESSMENT
1. Non-ST Elevation Myocardial Infarction (NSTEMI), suspected (I21.4) — ST depression and rising troponin in high-risk chest pain presentation.
2. Hypertension (I10) — Chronic, BP elevated on presentation.
3. Hyperlipidemia (E78.5) — Chronic, on statin.

PLAN
1. Aspirin 325 mg PO given in ED. Heparin infusion started per ACS protocol.
2. Cardiology consult placed — STAT. Dr. Huang paged.
3. Repeat troponin at 3 hours. Continuous cardiac monitoring.
4. Nitroglycerin 0.4 mg SL x1 given — chest pain improved to 3/10.
5. Metoprolol 25 mg PO administered.
6. Admit to Cardiac Care Unit (CCU) for further workup and possible cardiac catheterization.
7. NPO after midnight.

Follow-up: Inpatient admission — cardiology to manage.

Electronically signed: Dr. Rebecca Okafor, MD, FACEP — 2025-11-03 03:45
